mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal is continuously evaluated without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month Rank IC of monthly change in PAYEMS against 20-day forward returns is less than 0.05, or positive returns in months with payroll growth above median are not significantly higher than below median (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, it signals emerging labor market weakness that reduces consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week (5 trading days).
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return after UNRATE crosses above 5.0% is not negative, or the signal's hit rate (negative returns) is below 50% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The real interest rate, captured by the joint movement of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL (inflation), drives equity valuations. A rising real rate (Fed funds increasing faster than inflation) tightens financial conditions and suppresses stock prices. Conversely, a falling real rate supports equities. The signal is always active but requires both series.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of the real rate change (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL inflation) against 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 in absolute value over any 3-year window, or the direction is consistently positive when real rate rises.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low and falling unemployment (UNRATE below 5% and declining over 3 months), increases in retail sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer demand that drives corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
regime_hint: low unemployment expansion with improving labor market
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, the average 60-day forward return is not positive, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is below 0.5 over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals contraction in the manufacturing sector and broader economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month (20 trading days). The signal activates when the 12-month change in INDPRO turns negative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY change negative (based on most recent monthly value)
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return after INDPRO YoY turns negative is not negative, or the signal fails to predict negative returns with a hit rate above 55% across all historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad-based demand expansion driven by the housing wealth effect and consumer optimism, strongly supporting equity prices over the next 20 trading days. The interaction of these two demand proxies amplifies the signal.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS rising month-over-month
failure_condition: Average 20-day return following months with both series rising is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal is no better than a simple buy-and-hold over the same periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) are a direct measure of consumer spending, which constitutes the majority of GDP. Sustained growth in RSAFS signals healthy economic activity and supports corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This is a continuous, always-on signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC of monthly change in RSAFS against 20-day forward returns is less than 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window, or the predictive power is subsumed by other macro variables in a multiple regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above a threshold of 0.75% signals a tightening monetary policy stance that increases the discount rate on equities and reduces economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return after FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.75% is not negative, or the signal's hit rate for negative returns is below 55% across all historical occurrences.
